paper_title: Study of Sedimentary Depositions in the Gotland Deep

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from the Gotland Deep and storing them for further analysis.

steps.description: During a research cruise with RV Maria S. Merian in summer 2010, an 11.6 m long gravity core, together with a 'Frahmlot' core to obtain undisturbed surface layers, were taken in the Gotland Deep.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment cores using gravity and Frahmlot corers

steps.description: The core was stored cool on board and in the laboratory until sampling in February 2011.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment cores in cool conditions

methods.description: Preparation of sediment samples for elemental and isotopic analysis, including homogenization, decarbonation, and analysis of carbon, nitrogen, and sulfur content.

steps.description: The samples were homogenised by grinding, and aliquots were taken for elemental analyses (C / N / S) using a Eurovector Euro EA CNS analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize samples and prepare aliquots for elemental analysis

steps.description: To determine the contents of organic carbon (C org ), each sample was also analysed after decarbonatisation with HCl.
steps.searchable_summary: decarbonatize samples with HCl and analyze for organic carbon

steps.description: Bulk δ 15 N isotope analysis was carried out in duplicate using elemental analysis-isotope ratio mass spectrometry (EA-IRMS, Delta plus, Thermo scientific).
steps.searchable_summary: perform δ 15 N isotope analysis using EA-IRMS

methods.description: Determination of organic matter content in sediment samples by ashing and calculating mass difference.

steps.description: The loss on ignition (LOI) was determined in high resolution for the upper 500 cm of the gravity core by ashing freeze-dried samples at 550°C (3 h) and calculating the resulting mass difference (wt.%).
steps.searchable_summary: perform LOI analysis by ashing samples at 550°C and calculate mass difference
